title: BombaProMax WPF — Shell Polish Log
purpose: Machine-readable companion to docs/plans/shell-polish-recap.md. Lists every shell-polish issue (chronological), its root cause, and the exact fix that landed.
workflow: Diagnose -> wait for user 'GO' -> apply minimal fix -> build -> mark done.

issues.title: Remove placeholder pages
issues.symptom: 9 stub *Page.xaml files generated under BombaProMaxWPF/Views/Pages/ — wrong scope and wrong folder convention (project rule: Views/[Name]Pages/[Name]View.xaml).
issues.rootCause: First scaffolding pass preemptively created 9 nav pages.
fix: Deleted 18 files (xaml + xaml.cs) under BombaProMaxWPF/Views/Pages/ and removed the folder.
Removed PageType from Models/NavItem.cs.
Cleaned ViewModels/ShellViewModel.BuildItems (no typeof(...) args, no using BombaProMaxWPF.Views.Pages;).
MainWindow.OnNavigationRequested reduced to TODO no-op stub.

issues.title: Outer grid frame around content
issues.symptom: Visible decorative frame surrounded the content area.
issues.rootCause: Body Grid carried Margin="16,8,16,16" painted by an outer container.
fix: MainWindow.xaml body Grid Margin '16,8,16,16' -> '0'.
Pushed margins onto children: sidebar Margin='16,8,8,16', content grid Margin='8,8,16,16'.

issues.title: Language switch flips the whole layout
issues.symptom: Picking Arabic mirrored the entire UI (sidebar moves to right, icons reverse).
issues.rootCause: LanguageComboBox_SelectionChanged set FlowDirection=RightToLeft for 'ar'. FlowDirection cascades through every child by inheritance.
fix: Removed FlowDirection assignment from LanguageComboBox_SelectionChanged; only LanguageManager.Instance.SetLanguage(code) runs.
Localized hard-coded strings: tooltips (TogglePaneTooltip, LogoutTooltip), CTA (StartDay), and 9 nav titles.

Added 12 keys to Resources/Strings.resx and Strings.ar.resx: TogglePaneTooltip, StartDay, LogoutTooltip, NavDashboard, NavVentes, NavAchats, NavCaisse, NavClients, NavInfrastructure, NavRessources, NavRapports, NavParametres.
Models/NavItem now implements INotifyPropertyChanged and re-raises Title on LanguageManager.LanguageChanged.
issues.rule: Language switch never flips FlowDirection; only translates strings via LanguageManager resource indexer.

issues.title: Theme toggle background glitch (redo)
issues.symptom: Toggle Dark -> only window background paints dark; cards/sidebar/toolbar stay Light. Toggle back -> window background stuck Black.
rootCause: WPF auto-freezes SolidColorBrush instances when consumed by Style.Setter; the Color DP becomes immutable, so swapping Neu*Color keys at runtime is silently ignored.
Wpf.Ui.ApplicationThemeManager.Apply writes FluentWindow.Background as a local DP value, which beats DynamicResource markup.
fix: Theme/ThemePalette.cs Apply(bool dark) now writes 6 fresh frozen SolidColorBrush instances into Application.Current.Resources: NeuBackgroundBrush, NeuAccentBrush, NeuAccentHoverBrush, NeuTextPrimaryBrush, NeuTextSecondaryBrush, NeuInputFillBrush. Replacing the brush instance defeats the freeze.
issues.encodingGotcha: PowerShell Get-Content -Raw | -replace | Set-Content corrupts non-ASCII (e.g. '●' U+25CF in PasswordChar). Use [System.IO.File]::ReadAllText/WriteAllText with explicit UTF-8 BOM, or replace literal with XML entity '●'.

issues.title: Collapsed sidebar must hide labels
issues.symptom: Collapse toggle didn't hide brand text, CTA label, nav labels, footer text/role.
issues.rootCause: UpdateCollapsedVisuals only narrowed the column; inner labels and icons kept their expanded layout.
fix: Added x:Names in MainWindow.xaml: PaneToggleButton, StartDayIcon, NavIcon (in DataTemplate), UserAvatar, LogoutButton.

Rewrote MainWindow.xaml.cs.UpdateCollapsedVisuals to (collapsed): hide SidebarBrand/UserText/StartDayLabel/LogoutButton/every NavLabel; center PaneToggleButton; zero StartDayIcon.Margin; zero UserAvatar.Margin and center it; zero each NavIcon.Margin and center it; nav RadioButton.HorizontalContentAlignment=Center. All restored on expand.

issues.title: Collapsed sidebar over-clipped (icons invisible)
issues.symptom: First collapsed pass at 64 px showed only thin vertical accent strips; no icons visible.
issues.rootCause: Compounding horizontal padding ate 60 px before any icon: outer ContentControl Margin '16,8,8,16' (24 px) + NeuSidebarStyle.Padding=12 (24 px) + inner header/CTA/footer Margin '6,...,6,...' (12 px). Plus inner button styles: NeuStartDayButtonStyle.Padding='16,10' and NeuNavItemStyle.Padding='14,10'.
fix: SidebarCollapsedWidth: 64 -> 88 px.
UpdateCollapsedVisuals (collapsed): StartDayButton.Padding='0,10,0,10' (was '16,10' from style); each nav RadioButton.Padding='0,10,0,10' (was '14,10'). Both restored on expand.

issues.title: Selected nav: indicator butts icon, HoverFill offset
issues.symptom: Collapsed sidebar shows icons but selected row's 3 px indicator butts the icon with no spacing, and the rounded HoverFill highlight is offset to the right of the icon.
rootCause: NeuNavItemStyle template hard-coded ContentPresenter HorizontalAlignment='Stretch' which overrode RadioButton.HorizontalContentAlignment=Center set in code-behind, so inner DataTemplate Grid (Auto/*) kept icon glued to col 0.
HoverFill Margin '8,0,2,0' is asymmetric; visual center is 3 px right of true row center.
fix: Styles/Neumorphic.Light.xaml NeuNavItemStyle template: HoverFill.Margin '8,0,2,0' -> '6,0,6,0'.
Same template: ContentPresenter.HorizontalAlignment 'Stretch' -> '{TemplateBinding HorizontalContentAlignment}'.

issues.title: Burger placement clipped + focus halo, footer avatar clipped
issues.symptom: Burger has dashed focus halo and looks clipped/off-center; footer pill shows only a thin blue arc — avatar clipped to a sliver.
rootCause: Burger sits in col 1 (Auto) of a 2-col header Grid '*/Auto'. With 88 px sidebar, header has ~28 px — less than button Width=36 -> overflows/clips on the left. HorizontalAlignment=Center only centers within col 1 (= button width, no-op).
WPF default Button.FocusVisualStyle (dashed rect) was never overridden in NeuPaneToggleStyle.

Footer pill: sidebar inner = 40 px, UserPill.Margin '6,12,6,4' eats 12, NeuUserPillStyle.Padding '10,8' eats 20 -> 8 px content for 32 px avatar. Inner Grid Auto/*/Auto with col 1 (*) absorbing leftover space -> avatar pulled left.
fix: NeuPaneToggleStyle: added <Setter Property='FocusVisualStyle' Value='{x:Null}' /> to kill dashed halo.

UpdateCollapsedVisuals (collapsed): Grid.SetColumn(PaneToggleButton,0) + Grid.SetColumnSpan(PaneToggleButton,2) + HorizontalAlignment=Center; restored to Column=1, ColumnSpan=1, HorizontalAlignment=Right on expand.
UserPill.Padding '10,8,10,8' -> '0,8,0,8' collapsed.
UserAvatar (collapsed): Grid.SetColumnSpan=3, Width=Height=24, Margin=0, HorizontalAlignment=Center. Expanded restored: ColumnSpan=1, 32x32, Margin '0,0,10,0', HorizontalAlignment=Left.

lessonsLearned: WPF SolidColorBrush instances auto-freeze when used in style setters. Color-key swaps don't repaint — you must replace the brush instance itself.
Wpf.Ui.ApplicationThemeManager.Apply writes FluentWindow.Background as a local DP value. Use SetResourceReference after every Apply to re-attach the DynamicResource.
Hard-coded HorizontalAlignment='Stretch' on a ContentPresenter swallows HorizontalContentAlignment setters. Always bind via {TemplateBinding HorizontalContentAlignment} in custom templates.
Compounding padding layers (outer Margin -> style Padding -> inner Margin -> button Padding) can eat 60+ px before any icon. Never just widen the column — also strip inner padding when collapsed.
Inner Grid layouts with '*' columns trap children at the left edge regardless of HorizontalAlignment on the child. Use Grid.ColumnSpan to span all cols when you need genuine centering.
WPF default Button.FocusVisualStyle (dashed rect) is active unless explicitly overridden. Set FocusVisualStyle='{x:Null}' on icon-only buttons in custom styles.
Project localization rule: language switch never flips FlowDirection; LanguageManager indexer binding must be present on every view.

Encoding gotcha: PowerShell Get-Content -Raw with -replace corrupts non-ASCII characters. Use [System.IO.File]::ReadAllText/WriteAllText with explicit UTF-8 BOM, or use XML entities like ● for literal special chars.
